definitions.text: Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
examples: Did you take out the trash? — No, I did not. I do not think it was my turn, was it?
Not knowing any better, I went ahead.
That is not red; it's green.

definitions.text: Used to indicate the opposite or near opposite, often in a form of understatement.
examples: That day was not the best day of my life. (meaning the day was bad or awful)
It was not my favorite movie of all time. (meaning the speaker dislikes or strongly dislikes the movie)
I wanted a plate of shrimp, not a bucket of chicken.
He painted the car blue and black, not solid purple.

definitions.text: Used to indicate that the previous phrase was meant sarcastically or ironically.
examples: I really like hanging out with my little brother watching Barney … not!
Sure, you’re perfect the way you are … not!

definitions.text: An instance of using the word “not”; a negation or denial.
Alternative letter-case form of NOT (“unary operation on logical values that changes true to false, and false to true”).
